title_guess: Sussex: Hove (Gravestocks), Washington campsite, Bramber Castle, Brighton Pavilion/Pier

pages.text:
__April 10th  1999  Saturday__

John sang magnificently as Miles in the Turn of the Screw on Friday, & on Saturday at 9 am we set off for Sussex. We left Tonic [?at] kennels to be plucked, & continued on  to the Gravestocks in __Hove__. Jonathan, Charlotte, Abigail & Tom gave us a delicious lunch. We saw their tortoises, 2 baby tortoises, border terries, cat & aviary.  After the lunch we saw Jonathan's PDSA clinic, v. interesting, operating rooms, pills. 4 ½ vets. A dead kitten.  We walked & the boys rollerbladed in Preston Park  We returned to the Gravestocks for a cup of tea & Hot Cross Buns, before leaving for a campsite.
     Passing Lancing College, we came to Steyning — v. pretty, but only one small hut with one loo & cold water basin, so we drove on to __Washington__ lovely clean modern loo block, continental, infra-red light comes on as you walk in. Expensive. Foot paths.  Thomas is practising his

violin in the bushes.  Expensive £18 per night
     __April 11th__  Glorious sunshine, breakfast at a convenient picnic table next to the van.  John & Charles have gone exploring.  John has already got one leg soaked in the stream (milky white from the chalk)

[Hand-drawn sketch: architectural details labelled "Brighton Pavillion" showing emblem under ceiling frize, and serpent around palm pillar — labelled "Salton"]

We sat around the campsite until 11.30, then stopped at __Bramber Castle__, an old Norman castle, steep hill sides, outer wall & keep hill, not much left standing, except a few flint walls. Pretty.
        On to the sea front where we had lunch. A white car got bogged down in the shingle. Simon, Thomas, William & Peter worked for half-an-hour to help get it out, finally succeeding with a strip of webbing which Thomas found.
        The Pavillion was absolutely marvellous. Weird & fantastical, dragons, gold, Indian on the outside & Chinese on the inside. 1810. Oh George IV, while Prince Regent.
        After this we went out on __Brighton__ Pier, where all 5 boys went on __Dodgems__. John went on a ghastly high spinning machine too. The sea was a beautiful aquamarine colour. Windy.
        Back to the van & back to the campsite for a pub supper.
